text.title: pytest Parametrize — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-pytest-parametrize is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.reason: Root cause: CI says 'test_x[case3]' — unreadable. Fix: Add ids= or pytest.param(id='...').

antipattern.description: Three stacked parametrize each with 10 values = 1000 cases.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Suite runs in 10 minutes; each PR re-runs. Fix: Cap dimensions; pick representative values.

antipattern.description: Same parametrize covers 'valid email' and 'malformed unicode in name' — totally different assertions.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Test body grows into conditional branches. Fix: Split into two parametrize tests by behaviour.

antipattern.description: Parametrize that checks (input, status, body, header) all at once.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: First failure masks the rest; debugging unclear. Fix: Split into focused parametrize per assertion type.
